_REVIEW_NOTES.PURPOSE: Maps every English country name used in any data/*.json file to its standard Norwegian Bokmål equivalent. Sort order: alphabetical by English name. Notes on ambiguous entries are recorded in this section — the English key is the field name, value is the note.
_REVIEW_NOTES.Bohemia: Historical state (appeared in early Olympic data). 'Böhmen' is the standard Norwegian form. Not a current country — no flag image exists for it.
_REVIEW_NOTES.British West Indies: Historical grouping (early Olympic data). 'Britisk Vestindia' used here. Not a current country.
_REVIEW_NOTES.Czech Republic: The short official English name is now 'Czechia'; Norwegian equivalents are 'Den tsjekkiske republikk' (full) and 'Tsjekkia' (short). This file uses 'Tsjekkia', which is the dominant Norwegian news/school form.
_REVIEW_NOTES.Democratic Republic of the Congo: 'Den demokratiske republikken Kongo' is the full formal name. Some Norwegian sources use 'Kongo-Kinshasa' to disambiguate from the Republic of the Congo. Full name used here.
_REVIEW_NOTES.Eswatini: Renamed from Swaziland in 2018. 'Eswatini' is now the accepted Norwegian form.
_REVIEW_NOTES.Hong Kong: Special Administrative Region of China. Written as one word 'Hongkong' in Norwegian.
_REVIEW_NOTES.Ivory Coast: Official French name 'Côte d'Ivoire' is sometimes used in Norwegian formal contexts, but 'Elfenbenskysten' is the standard Bokmål translation and is used in Norwegian schools and news.
_REVIEW_NOTES.Micronesia: Full name is 'Mikronesiaføderasjonen' (Federated States of Micronesia). Shortened to 'Mikronesia' here for display purposes — let me know if you prefer the full form.
_REVIEW_NOTES.Netherlands Antilles: Dissolved 2010 (appears in older Olympic data). 'De nederlandske Antiller' used here.
_REVIEW_NOTES.Republic of the Congo: 'Kongo' or 'Republikken Kongo' are both used in Norwegian. 'Kongo-Brazzaville' is common in news to distinguish from the DRC. This file uses 'Kongo' — flag it if you want the longer form.
_REVIEW_NOTES.Timor-Leste: The official English/Portuguese name is 'Timor-Leste'. The traditional Norwegian name is 'Øst-Timor'. Norwegian news increasingly uses 'Timor-Leste'. This file uses 'Øst-Timor' as the school/common form — let me know if you prefer 'Timor-Leste'.
_REVIEW_NOTES.United States: Colloquially 'USA' in Norwegian (and on Norwegian TV/news/schools). The full form is 'De forente stater'. 'USA' used here.
_REVIEW_NOTES.Virgin Islands: Likely the US Virgin Islands in this dataset. Norwegian: 'Jomfruøyene' (short) or 'De amerikanske Jomfruøyene' (full). Short form used here.
